questions.stem: What is the main purpose of a PivotTable?
options: To summarise and reorganise large amounts of data interactively
To protect a workbook with a password
questions.explanation: A PivotTable lets you summarise, group and rearrange large lists of data quickly, turning raw records into a compact report you can reshape by dragging fields.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: From which Ribbon tab do you insert a PivotTable?
questions.explanation: The PivotTable command sits in the Tables group on the Insert tab.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A PivotTable has four drop areas in its field pane. Which set lists them correctly?
questions.explanation: The PivotTable field pane has four areas: Filters, Columns, Rows and Values, into which you drag fields to build the report.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Where should you drag a field so its categories appear as the row labels down the left side of the report?
questions.explanation: A field placed in the Rows area becomes the row labels, listing each of its unique categories down the left side of the PivotTable.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which area should hold the numeric field you want to total or average?
questions.explanation: Numbers to be aggregated, such as sales or quantities, go into the Values area, where Excel summarises them with a function like Sum or Average.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What does a field placed in the Filters area let you do?
options: Filter the entire report from a drop-down above the table
questions.explanation: A field in the Filters area creates a report filter drop-down above the PivotTable that restricts the whole report to the chosen category or categories.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: By default, how does a PivotTable summarise a numeric field dropped into the Values area?
questions.explanation: When a field contains numbers, the Values area summarises it with Sum by default. You can change this through Value Field Settings.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You drag a text field such as Product Name into the Values area. Which summary does Excel use by default?
questions.explanation: Text and other non-numeric fields cannot be summed, so the Values area defaults to Count, reporting how many entries fall into each grouping.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You place Region in Rows, Month in Columns, and Sales in Values. What does the body of the PivotTable show?
options: Total sales for each region and month combination
A list of every individual sale
questions.explanation: With Region as rows and Month as columns, each body cell shows the summarised Sales for that region in that month, forming a region by month matrix of totals.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: After you change a value in the source data, does the PivotTable update on its own?
options: No, you must refresh the PivotTable
Only if you save the file
questions.explanation: A PivotTable works from a snapshot of the source, so edits to the data do not appear until you refresh the PivotTable to reload the latest values.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which action refreshes a PivotTable to pick up changes in the source data?
options: Right-click the PivotTable and choose Refresh
questions.explanation: Right-clicking inside the PivotTable and choosing Refresh, or using the Refresh button on the PivotTable Analyze tab, reloads the data and recalculates the summary.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Through which dialog do you change a value field from Sum to Average?
questions.explanation: Value Field Settings, reached by clicking the value field and choosing it, lets you switch the summary function among Sum, Average, Count, Max, Min and others.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What is the recommended structure for the source data before creating a PivotTable?
options: A single header row with no blank columns and no blank rows
questions.explanation: Clean source data has one row of unique column headings and no empty columns or rows, so Excel can recognise each field and include every record.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What happens when you double-click a single value cell inside a PivotTable?
options: Excel drills through and lists the underlying source rows on a new sheet
questions.explanation: Double-clicking a value cell performs a drill-through, creating a new worksheet that shows the detailed source records that make up that summarised figure.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What is a Slicer in the context of PivotTables?
options: A set of clickable buttons that filter the PivotTable visually
questions.explanation: A Slicer is a panel of buttons, one per field item, that filters the PivotTable with a click and clearly shows which items are currently selected.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What is a PivotChart?
options: A chart that cannot be filtered
A chart linked to a PivotTable that updates as the pivot changes
questions.explanation: A PivotChart is a graphical view tied to a PivotTable. Rearranging or filtering the pivot updates the chart, and the chart offers its own field buttons.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You want to total Sales and also show the average Sales in the same PivotTable. How can you do this?
options: Add the Sales field to the Values area twice and set one to Sum and the other to Average
Convert the table to a range
questions.explanation: The same field can be dropped into the Values area more than once, letting you display Sum of Sales and Average of Sales side by side after adjusting each value field setting.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: How can you summarise a Date field into months or quarters within a PivotTable?
options: Use the Group feature on the date field
questions.explanation: Grouping a date field lets you roll daily dates up into months, quarters or years, so the PivotTable reports at a coarser time scale.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: If your source list grows with new rows beyond the original range, what should you do so the PivotTable includes them?
options: Use Change Data Source to extend the range, then refresh
questions.explanation: When rows are added outside the captured range, Change Data Source on the PivotTable Analyze tab lets you point to the larger range, and a refresh then brings in the new records.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: In the value header, what does a label such as Count of Orders tell you?
options: The field Orders is summarised using the Count function
questions.explanation: The value header names the summary function and the field, so Count of Orders means the Orders field is being counted rather than summed or averaged.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What does the PivotTable Fields pane display?
options: A list of the source columns as fields you can drag into the areas
questions.explanation: The PivotTable Fields pane lists every column from the source data as a field, and you build the report by dragging these fields into the Filters, Rows, Columns and Values areas.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which summary function would you choose in Value Field Settings to find the largest value in each group?
questions.explanation: The Max function reports the highest value within each grouping, which is useful for spotting peak sales or the top score per category.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Moving a field from the Rows area to the Columns area has what effect?
options: It changes the summary to Count
Its categories shift from running down the side to running across the top
questions.explanation: Dragging a field from Rows to Columns pivots its categories from vertical row labels into horizontal column headings, reshaping how the summary is laid out.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What do the Grand Total row and column in a PivotTable show?
options: The overall totals across all rows and all columns
questions.explanation: The Grand Total row sums each column and the Grand Total column sums each row, giving the overall totals for the whole report. They can be turned off if not wanted.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible
